channel.title: Blog.elmah.io on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Blog.elmah.io on .NET Ramblings

item.title: How .NET handles exceptions internally (and why they're expensive)
item.description: What really happens when you write throw new Exception() in .NET? Microsoft guidelines state that When a member throws an exception, its performance can be orders of magnitude slower. It's not just a simple jump to a catch block, but a lot goes in CLR (Common Language Runtime) Link to article: https://blog.elmah.io/how-net-handles-exceptions-internally-and-why-theyre-expensive/
